steps.title: State the y-intercept rule
steps.content: The $y$-intercept of a line in the $xy$-plane is the point where the line crosses the $y$-axis. At that point $x = 0$, so the $y$-coordinate of the $y$-intercept equals the value of $y$ when $x = 0$.

steps.title: Read the value from the table
steps.content: From the given table, the row where $x = 0$ shows $y = 15$. So the $y$-coordinate of the $y$-intercept is $\boxed{15}$.
